questions.question: What does `justify-items` control in CSS Grid?
options: Horizontal alignment of items within their cells
Vertical alignment of items within their cells
Horizontal alignment of tracks in the container
Vertical alignment of tracks in the container
questions.explanation: `justify-items` controls horizontal (inline/row axis) alignment of grid items within their assigned cells.

questions.question: What is the default value for `align-items` on a grid container?
questions.explanation: The default for `align-items` (and `justify-items`) in CSS Grid is `stretch`, meaning items fill their entire cell.

questions.question: What's the difference between `justify-items` and `justify-content`?
options: justify-items aligns items in cells; justify-content aligns tracks in the container
justify-items is for rows; justify-content is for columns
questions.explanation: `justify-items` controls how items behave within their grid cells. `justify-content` controls how the grid tracks are positioned within the container.

questions.question: When does `justify-content` have a visible effect?
options: When the total track width is smaller than the container
Only when the container has a background
questions.explanation: `justify-content` (and `align-content`) only have a visible effect when the grid tracks are smaller than the container. If tracks fill the container, there's nothing to distribute.

questions.question: How do you center a single grid item in its cell?
questions.explanation: `place-self: center` is the shorthand for `align-self: center; justify-self: center;` and centers a single item in its grid cell.

questions.question: Which shorthand property covers both `align-items` and `justify-items`?
questions.explanation: `place-items` is a shorthand that sets both `align-items` and `justify-items`. `place-items: center` centers items on both axes.

questions.question: What does `justify-content: space-evenly` do?
options: Distributes tracks with equal space between them and at the edges
Distributes tracks with space only between them
questions.explanation: `space-evenly` distributes the tracks with equal space between each track and also between the outermost tracks and the container edges.

questions.question: Which property would you use to make a single item stick to the bottom-right of its cell?
questions.explanation: Both `place-self: end end` (shorthand) and `justify-self: end; align-self: end` (longhand) position an item at the bottom-right of its cell.

questions.question: What is the difference between `space-between` and `space-around`?
options: space-between: no space at edges; space-around: half-space at edges
space-between: equal space; space-around: unequal space
questions.explanation: `space-between` puts all extra space between tracks with none at edges. `space-around` puts half the space at each edge and full space between tracks.

questions.question: If you set `justify-items: center` on a container, can a single item align to the right?
options: Yes â€” use `justify-self: end` on that item
No â€” the container setting applies to all items
Only if the item has a fixed width
questions.explanation: `justify-self` on an individual item overrides the container's `justify-items` setting for that item only.

questions.question: What does `place-content: center` do?
options: Centers the entire grid in the container on both axes
questions.explanation: `place-content: center` is shorthand for `align-content: center; justify-content: center;`, centering the grid tracks within the container.

questions.question: Why might `justify-content: center` appear to have no effect?
options: Tracks are using `fr` units that fill the container â€” there's no space to distribute
questions.explanation: When using `fr` units, the tracks fill the entire container, leaving no extra space for content alignment to act on.

questions.question: What values are valid for `justify-content` but NOT for `justify-items`?
questions.explanation: Distribution values (`space-between`, `space-around`, `space-evenly`) are exclusive to content alignment properties (`justify-content`/`align-content`). Item alignment uses `start`, `end`, `center`, `stretch`, and `baseline`.

questions.question: What does `align-self: baseline` do?
options: Aligns the item's text baseline with other items in the same row
Aligns the item to the bottom of its cell
Aligns the item to the grid baseline
Sets a baseline for responsive design
questions.explanation: `baseline` alignment aligns the text baseline of grid items within the same row, which is useful for cards with varying content heights.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a valid value for `justify-items`?
questions.explanation: `space-between` is a content distribution value, available on `justify-content`/`align-content` but not on item alignment properties.

questions.question: If an item has `width: 50px` in a grid cell of 100px, what alignment property controls its horizontal position?
questions.explanation: `justify-self` controls horizontal alignment within the cell. With `width: 50px` in a 100px cell, you can use `justify-self: start`, `center`, or `end`.

questions.question: What does `place-items: center stretch` do?
options: Centers items vertically, stretches them horizontally
questions.explanation: `place-items` is `align-items justify-items`. So `place-items: center stretch` means `align-items: center` (vertical center) and `justify-items: stretch` (horizontal stretch).

questions.question: In a 500px container with 3 columns of 100px each, which content alignment creates the most space between columns?
questions.explanation: `space-between` puts ALL remaining space (500 - 300 = 200px) between the three columns (100px between each pair), giving the widest gaps.

questions.question: What does `align-content: end` do in a grid taller than its tracks?
options: Pushes all the grid tracks to the bottom of the container
Aligns items to the end of their cells
Ends the grid at the bottom
Aligns the last row of items
questions.explanation: `align-content: end` (when tracks total less than container height) pushes all grid tracks to the bottom of the container, leaving space at the top.

questions.question: Which is the correct way to center both items AND the grid?
options: place-items: center on items; place-content: center on the grid in container
Only one can be centered at a time
questions.explanation: These solve different problems. `place-items: center` centers items within their cells. `place-content: center` centers the tracks within the container. Use both for complete centering.
